title: Jabalpur Madan Mahal
tagline: Jabalpur Madan Mahal — heritage city of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Madan Mahal is a suburban area of Jabalpur famous for the historical Durgavati fort.
overview.description: Madan Mahal is a suburban area of Jabalpur famous for the historical Durgavati fort. The area also has a railway station named Madan Mahal. Madan Mahal Fort in Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh, stands as a testimony to the lives of the rulers who ruled Jabalpur for a good number of years in the 14th century AD. Situated at a hilltop, a couple of kilometres away from the city, Madan Mahal Fort was built by the Raja Madan Singh.

topPlaces.name: Pisanhari Ki Marhia
topPlaces.description: Pisanhari ki Marhia is a Jain temple built in the 15th century and located in the city of Jabalpur in Madhya Pradesh, India. The temple is named after its creator, a local woman who, according to legend, paid for the construction of the temple with the money she saved from milling flour.

topPlaces.name: Ravishankar Shukla Stadium
topPlaces.description: Ravi Shankar Shukla Stadium is the main sports venue in Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh. It is used for several sports, like association football and cricket.

topPlaces.name: Jubbulpore Division
topPlaces.description: The Jubbulpore Division, named after its capital Jabalpur (Jubbulpore), was one of the four former administrative divisions of the Central Provinces of British India. It was located in the Mahakoshal region of present-day Madhya Pradesh state of India.

topPlaces.name: Mahakoshal
topPlaces.description: Mahakoshal or Mahakaushal is a region of central India. Mahakoshal lies in the upper or eastern reaches of the Narmada River valley in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Pandit Ravishankar Shukla Stadium
topPlaces.description: Ravishankar Shukla Stadium is a cricket ground in Durg, Chhattisgarh. The hosted its first match between Madhya Pradesh cricket team against Uttar Pradesh cricket team in 1979.

topPlaces.name: Rani Durgawati Museum
topPlaces.description: Rani Durgawati Museum is a museum in Jabalpur city in Madhya Pradesh state of India. It was established in 1976.

topPlaces.name: Jabalpur
topPlaces.description: Jabalpur (IPA: [d͡ʒəbəlpʊɾ]), formerly anglicised as Jubbulpore, is a city situated on the banks of the Narmada River in the state of Madhya Pradesh, India. Jabalpur is the administrative headquarters of the Jabalpur district and the Jabalpur division.

topPlaces.name: Hanumantal Bada Jain Mandir
topPlaces.description: Bara Mandir (हनुमान-ताल मन्दिर) is a historic Jain temple in Jabalpur, India, right on the edge of Hanumantal, once the main center of Jabalpur.

items.note: Pisanhari ki Marhia is a Jain temple built in the 15th century and located in the city of Jabalpur in Madhya Pradesh, India. The temple is named after its creator, a local woman who, according to legend, paid for the construction of the temple with the money she saved from milling flour.
Ravi Shankar Shukla Stadium is the main sports venue in Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh. It is used for several sports, like association football and cricket.

The Jubbulpore Division, named after its capital Jabalpur (Jubbulpore), was one of the four former administrative divisions of the Central Provinces of British India. It was located in the Mahakoshal region of present-day Madhya Pradesh state of India.
Mahakoshal or Mahakaushal is a region of central India. Mahakoshal lies in the upper or eastern reaches of the Narmada River valley in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.
Ravishankar Shukla Stadium is a cricket ground in Durg, Chhattisgarh. The hosted its first match between Madhya Pradesh cricket team against Uttar Pradesh cricket team in 1979.
Rani Durgawati Museum is a museum in Jabalpur city in Madhya Pradesh state of India. It was established in 1976.

Jabalpur (IPA: [d͡ʒəbəlpʊɾ]), formerly anglicised as Jubbulpore, is a city situated on the banks of the Narmada River in the state of Madhya Pradesh, India. Jabalpur is the administrative headquarters of the Jabalpur district and the Jabalpur division.
Bara Mandir (हनुमान-ताल मन्दिर) is a historic Jain temple in Jabalpur, India, right on the edge of Hanumantal, once the main center of Jabalpur.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jabalpur Madan Mahal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jabalpur Madan Mahal is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jabalpur Madan Mahal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jabalpur Airport (16 km) and the nearest railway station is Jabalpur Junction (4 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jabalpur Madan Mahal?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jabalpur Madan Mahal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jabalpur Madan Mahal?
faq.a: Stays in Jabalpur Madan Mahal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jabalpur Madan Mahal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pisanhari Ki Marhia, Ravishankar Shukla Stadium, Jubbulpore Division, Mahakoshal, Pandit Ravishankar Shukla Stadium.
